title: Rootful networking broken
body:
### Issue Description

Rootful networking broken on both my laptop and vps. On other side, rootless networking seems to work perfectly.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue:
1. Either deploy VPS with Fedora CoreOS 41.20250302.3.2 or install Fedora Kinoite 41.20250316.0,
2. Run any container in rootful mode,
3. Try to do any networking activity inside container.

### Describe the results you received

Networking doesn't work at all in rootful containers.

### Describe the results you expected

Networking works in rootful containers.

### podman info output

```yaml
VPS: https://pastebin.com/bgJvXtiS
Laptop: https://pastebin.com/p0G69ML7
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

VPS is arm64 with podman 5.4.0,
laptop is amd64 with podman 5.4.1.

All settings are at defaults.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: Hello @etidibeoz have you tried this on a regular Fedora (non-CoreOS) environment. This does work for me on a regular Fedora rawhide. Wonder if it's specific to coreos.

Did this use to work on is this the first time you tried it? Is so what was the last working version? And I agree please try to reproduce this elsewhere so we know this is not a coreos issue.

In general 

> Networking doesn't work at all in rootful containers.

isn't helpful to us. What have you tried to troubleshoot it? As root by default we use bridge networking which adds firewall rules to masquerade the traffic and have to enable routing (i.e. forwarding)

Please check you firewall rules and if forwarding is enabled and if the bridge + veth interfaces are properly configured.

> Did this use to work on is this the first time you tried it? Is so what was the last working version?

It used to work in the past. Version is unknown, as I was using just rootless containers for some time.

> And I agree please try to reproduce this elsewhere so we know this is not a coreos issue.

As I already mentioned, it also broken on my laptop with Fedora Kinoite (KDE Atomic). I tested it after noticing issue on vps with coreos.

> Please check you firewall rules

Empty rules on both laptop and coreos vps.

> and if forwarding is enabled

`net.ipv4.ip_forward = 1`

> and if the bridge + veth interfaces are properly configured.

Unsure how to properly check that.

After @Luap99 comment on forwarding found that problem is unrelated to podman. Thanks for clues.

1) There was a small mistake in firewall rules configuration on my vps.
2) For some unknown reason, firewall was dropping all forwarded packets. Same distro on friend's laptop has forwarding set to "accept". Both of us have never changed our firewall settings.

It was just a coincidence that problem was noticed simultaneously.

Are you using docker as well? I am aware of this https://docs.docker.com/engine/network/packet-filtering-firewalls/#docker-on-a-router bit sure if they are other tools that configure forward to drop by default.

> Are you using docker as well? I am aware of this https://docs.docker.com/engine/network/packet-filtering-firewalls/#docker-on-a-router bit sure if they are other tools that configure forward to drop by default.

Yes, I have docker on my laptop. So, it was docker's fault, then.

Probably it is worth to add a note about that behavior somewhere inside documentation.
